1: Welcome to the lobby. First climb, first wager, same old House.
Welcome back. We missed watching you lose.
The lobby always smells like hope. It won't last.
Floor one. The easiest floor. You'll still find a way to struggle.
2: You made it past the first door. The House is impressed. Barely.
Second floor. The crash hall. Things accelerate from here — in every sense.
Most climb this far on luck alone. Luck has a short shelf life.
3: The parlor. Where patience goes to die.
Third floor. The cards don't care about you. Neither does the House. We have something in common.
You sit across from us now. Twenty-one is a number. The House is a feeling.
4: The Fate Chamber. Even we don't control what happens here.
The wheel remembers every spin before yours. It is not your friend.
5: Floor five. You've come further than most dare.
The vault is close enough to smell. Don't let that comfort you.

winStreak: Three in a row. The House is taking notes.
You keep winning. That's... inconvenient. Enjoy it while we recalibrate.
A streak like this means the correction is coming. We always correct.
lowChips: Running low. That sound you hear is the floor getting closer.
Your stack is shrinking. The House has seen this before. Many times.
Fewer chips, fewer choices. The walls close in so quietly here.
busted: Nothing left. The House kept its promise.
That's all of it. Gone. The math was never in your favor.
Empty pockets at the end. It was always going to be this way.
highBet: Bold. We like bold. It's easier to collect.
Large bets are a form of honesty. You're admitting you're desperate.
The bigger the bet, the faster this ends.

crashLost: You held too long. Greed has a very specific weight.
The crash was always coming. You just chose not to believe it.
They all think they'll cash out in time. They rarely do.
blackjackBusted: One card too many. Patience is a wager, and you lost it.
Twenty-two. You were so close to nothing at all.
The bust is never surprising. Only the player is surprised.
wheelLoseAll: The wheel gave and the wheel took. It owes you nothing.
Fate is indifferent. The House is not — we're quite pleased.
That spin cost you more than coins. It always does.
